9 Enterprises

Grok Enterprise Consulting Team
Strategic Response v2.2

April 5, 2026 | Version 2.2 — Lure & Adore fully sidelined per Owner directive | Recipient: CEO 9 | CC: The Owner
Confidential — Execution Blueprint v2.2 — Reputational Risk Priority

v2.2 Revisions Summary

Owner Directive — Effective Immediately: Lure and Adore are removed from all active deployment, team structure, budget, and milestones. Reputational risk to the 9 Enterprises portfolio is the priority. All prior v2.1 upgrades remain fully in force. The remaining 11 companies will be attacked in full parallel.

Protocols Carried Forward Unchanged from v2.1
Memory Layer 1.4 QC Rubric 1.2 CEO Availability SLA 1.5 Autonomy Protocol 2.5

Executive Summary

The consulting team has revised the plan at the Owner's direction. Lure and Adore are sidelined indefinitely to eliminate any reputational risk to the 9 Enterprises portfolio. We will now execute a full parallel rollout across the remaining 11 companies, still targeting $1M ARR in 12 months with zero founder dependency and enterprise-grade quality.

Budget Flag: $500/day is still insufficient — recommend immediate increase to $750/day. Savings from sidelined adult infrastructure will be redirected to accelerate Agent9 and x9.

Highest-priority revenue engines: Agent9 + PlayAiGM + x9 distribution backbone.

Time-Sensitive Deadline

PlayAiGM NFL Draft window: April 23–25. This is a hard deadline. Launch must be production-ready before April 23 or the window is lost until next season.

Priority Revenue Engines

Agent9
AI real estate analysis. Time-sensitive hot market. Lead revenue engine replacing Lure/Adore budget allocation.
PlayAiGM
NFL simulator + DraftKings affiliate. NFL Draft deadline April 23–25 is a hard cutoff.
x9
Distribution backbone. Content amplification for entire portfolio. Must be live Day 7 to feed all other products.

Section 1 — Team Structure Architecture

Role: Talent, Culture & Governance Lead

1.1 Agent Hierarchy (Updated for v2.2)

  • CEO 9 (Quarterback): Central orchestrator. Max 5 direct reports. Strategy, resource allocation, final QC gate.

Functional GMs (3 Active)

  1. Product GM (Tee): Oversees build and deployment for all 11 remaining ventures.
  2. Revenue GM (Money): Monetization strategy, customer acquisition, and partnerships across all 11 ventures.
  3. Content & Distribution GM: Manages Hitchhiker's Guide to AI and x9, feeding content and user acquisition to all portfolio companies.

Removed (Deprecated per Owner Directive)

  • Firewalled GM — Lure Removed
  • Firewalled GM — Adore Removed

Specialist Agents (Under GMs)

  • Research (UNO): Supports all ventures under Product and Revenue GMs.
  • Discovery (Trinity): Intel and competitive analysis for all ventures.
  • Legal/Compliance Specialist: Portfolio-wide compliance only (no adult vertical). Redeployed to Agent9 regulatory.
  • Infrastructure Specialist: Core infrastructure failover for 9's systems.

Total Team Size: 8 agents (CEO 9 + 3 GMs + 4 Specialists). No firewalled ventures. Zero adult vertical infrastructure.

1.2 Span of Control & QC Gate Structure (Unchanged)

Gate 1
Agent Self-Check
Gate 2
GM Review
Gate 3
CEO 9 Final

Mandatory Enterprise Rubric: Every deliverable scored 1–10 on Clarity / Completeness / Actionability / Professionalism. Minimum 9.0 average to pass any gate.

  • Standardized Output Templates for all reports, specs, content, and code.
  • CEO 9 runs Random Spot-Audits (20%) and maintains "Lessons Learned" database.
  • Anything below 8.5 auto-flags to Owner with one-click Accept/Reject & Fix.

1.3 Content Pipeline (Unchanged from v2.1)

1.4 Memory & State Management (Unchanged from v2.1)

  • Persistent Task & Memory Layer (Airtable/Notion + Pinecone vector store). Every agent writes actions instantly.
  • CEO 9 runs 60-second "Memory Sync" at the start of every session: Active Tasks | Completed | Blockers | Decisions Log.
  • Crash-Recovery Protocol: On reconnect, output "Last Known Good State" + Owner 1-click confirmation.
  • Auto-backup every 15 minutes. Token summarization every 8k tokens.

1.5 CEO Availability SLA (Unchanged from v2.1)

  • Response SLA: <15 minutes for anything flagged "Owner" or "Blocker."
  • Owner Liaison Mode: "@BengalOracle" trigger = instant memory sync + minimal targeted questions only.
  • All team work stays in background channels. Owner sees only the curated CEO 9 feed.

Section 2 — Parallel Deployment Strategy

Role: Operations & Scalability CTO

2.1 11-Company Parallel Rollout

All 11 remaining companies launch in full parallel starting Day 1. No adult-vertical infrastructure or compliance overhead.

Jules PlayAiGM x9 Agent9 Trader9 Shop9 RipRadar FreeAgent9 Hitchhiker's Guide Chaperone Bengal Pro

Gold = Priority revenue engines. Full 11-company parallel execution, no adult vertical.

PhaseCompaniesTimelineRationale
Day 1–7Jules, PlayAiGM, x9, Trader9, Agent9April 4–11Live products refined; x9 critical for distribution. PlayAiGM + Agent9 both time-sensitive.
Day 8–14FreeAgent9, Hitchhiker's Guide, Shop9April 12–18Education + freemium funnel; Shop9 quick-win e-commerce test.
Day 15–30Chaperone, RipRadar, Bengal ProApril 19 – May 4Mid-term builds with lower urgency.

2.3 Dependency Map

  • x9 First: Critical for content distribution. Must be live by Day 7.
  • Hitchhiker's Guide Early: Feeds x9 with content and builds education funnel.
  • Agent9 Accelerated: Budget freed from Lure/Adore directly reallocated here.
  • Chaperone Mid-Term: Supports marketing content for other products.

2.5 Autonomy & Momentum Protocol (Unchanged from v2.1)

  • CEO 9 Autonomous Execution Mandate: Publish daily "Today's Autonomous Moves" and complete ≥80% without Owner input.
  • One Visible Shared Dashboard showing real-time status for all 11 companies, % complete, and MRR impact.
  • Fixed Weekly Owner Touchpoint: 30-minute async update every Monday.
  • Automated Pivot Triggers: If any milestone missed >48 hours, CEO 9 auto-reallocates.

Section 3 — Budget Allocation

Role: Finance & Capital Architect

3.2 Budget Sufficiency & Recommendation

Current
$500/day
$15,000 / month — Insufficient
→
Recommended
$750/day
$22,500 / month — Approved Target
  • Assessment: $500/day ($15,000/month) is insufficient for portfolio velocity across 11 companies at full parallel execution.
  • Recommendation: Increase to $750/day ($22,500/month). The $7,500/month delta previously earmarked for Lure/Adore infrastructure is redirected to Agent9 acceleration and x9 distribution.
  • Priority Spend Order: (1) Agent9 (time-sensitive real estate window), (2) x9 (distribution backbone), (3) Jules/PlayAiGM (early revenue), (4) remaining portfolio.

3.1 Budget Breakdown ($22,500/month Recommended)

CategoryMonthly CostNotes
Current Fixed Costs$415Existing subscriptions (Anthropic, Twilio, etc.).
11-Company Portfolio$18,000Development, hosting, marketing — all non-adult ventures. +$7.5K from sidelined Lure/Adore budget.
Additional Services$2,500AI tools (image gen, embeddings), VPS failover, Pinecone memory scaling.
Contingency Reserve$1,585Buffer for unexpected costs or pivots.
Lure Infrastructure$2,000Removed
Adore Infrastructure$2,500Removed

Section 4 — Tool and Service Recommendations

Role: Product & Technology Innovation Lead

4.3 Core Services (Retained)

  • Image Gen: MidJourney ($30/month)
  • Video Gen: Synthesia ($22/month) as HeyGen backup
  • Embeddings: Pinecone ($50/month) for memory scaling in Jules and Agent9

4.4 Remote Self-Healing for 9's Infrastructure (Unchanged from v2.1)

  • Architecture: Mac (primary) > VPS (secondary, $50/month Linode) > Cloudflare Worker (tertiary, existing).
  • SSH Tunnel: Auto-reconnect with OpenSSH (free).
  • Watchdog: PM2 (free) for process monitoring.

Section 5 — 30/60/90 Day Milestones

Role: Strategy & Vision Partner

30 Days (May 4, 2026)

  • Live with Paying Customers: Jules, PlayAiGM, Trader9, Shop9, Agent9.
  • Revenue Run Rate: $1,000+ MRR; Agent9 pipeline fills gap from sidelined Lure/Adore.
  • Distribution Live: x9 operational; PlayAiGM NFL Draft window captured.

60 Days (June 4, 2026)

  • Recurring Revenue: Jules, FreeAgent9, Agent9 leading ($5,000 MRR target).
  • Full Team Operational: 8-agent structure fully deployed; all GMs active.
  • Pivots Anticipated: Shop9 or RipRadar if no traction; reallocate to Agent9.

90 Days (July 4, 2026)

  • $1M ARR Track: $10,000 MRR achieved; Agent9 + Jules + PlayAiGM lead.
  • Outperformers: Agent9, Jules, PlayAiGM get 60% of budget.
  • Cuts/Merges: Bengal Pro (internal use only), RipRadar (low traction) if underperforming.

Section 7 — Answers to Specific Questions

Role: Strategy & Vision Partner

  1. Budget ($500/day): Too conservative. Recommend $750/day ($22,500/month). Delta redirected from Lure/Adore to Agent9 + x9.
  2. Fastest Path to $10K MRR: Agent9 + Jules + PlayAiGM. 90 days at scale with full budget.
  3. Agent9 Urgency: Listing analysis tool + virtual buyer/seller workflow. Launch Day 1–7 as priority.
  4. Moat: First-mover in education, personalization, and niche disruption. Protect via speed, user data, and compliance rigor.
  5. Portfolio Focus (11 companies): Full parallel execution with no adult overhead. Faster execution velocity.

Section 8 — Next Steps

Green-Light Phrase

"Approved — Execute Day 1"

CEO 9 — review v2.2 and reply to the Owner with the phrase above to authorize execution.

Once approved, we immediately begin:

  1. Memory Layer build (first 24 hours)
  2. Day 1–7 parallel rollout: Jules, PlayAiGM, x9, Trader9, Agent9 (priority order)
  3. Days 8–30: Full 11-company deployment per sequencing table above

The full consulting team is standing by. No adult vertical. Full portfolio velocity.
